description: Medici marketing site rebuild — outcome-focused copy, chat demo UI, structured pilot CTA, trust-building sections

userStories.title: Rewrite hero section copy
userStories.description: Rewrite the hero headline, status line, and CTAs to lead with outcomes (not technology). No mention of AI, intelligence, or machine learning above the fold.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero headline (h-large + h-italic lines) rewritten to focus on the WORK OUTCOME — what gets delivered, not the tool. Should emotionally resonate with a Fund CFO or PE Partner exhausted by manual work
The word 'AI' does NOT appear anywhere in the hero section
Status line changed from 'Live with funds in PE, credit, and real estate' to something with a concrete number or metric (e.g., documents processed, funds live)
h-sub line rewritten to list specific concrete deliverables (LP reports, K-1 packages, rent roll extractions, etc.)
Primary CTA remains 'Start a Pilot' linking to #contact
Secondary CTA changed to 'Watch it work ↓' (or similar downward-pointing text) linking to #product
ALL existing HTML structure preserved — same classes, same nesting, same element types. Only text content changes
hero-glyph, hero-dots, hero-accent-line, hero-scroll-hint completely untouched
userStories.notes: ONLY edit index.html lines 23-50. Touch NOTHING else. The tone should be confident and inevitable — 'this is how work should have always been' not 'try our exciting new tool.'

userStories.title: Add chat demo CSS to stylesheet
userStories.description: Add all the CSS needed for the new chat demo UI to css/styles.css. This is CSS ONLY — no HTML or JS changes yet.
acceptanceCriteria: New CSS classes added to styles.css for: .chat-frame, .chat-chrome, .chat-tabs, .chat-tab, .chat-tab.active, .chat-version, .chat-body, .chat-msg, .chat-msg.visible, .chat-msg.user, .chat-avatar, .chat-avatar.cosimo, .chat-avatar.user, .chat-bubble, .chat-msg.user .chat-bubble, .chat-msg.cosimo .chat-bubble, .chat-context, .chat-citations, .chat-cite, .chat-artifact, .chat-artifact-icon, .chat-artifact-info, .chat-artifact-name, .chat-artifact-meta, .chat-thinking, .chat-thinking-dot, @keyframes thinkPulse, .chat-input-bar, .chat-input-placeholder, .chat-try-cta, .chat-try-cta a
Chat frame is ALWAYS dark: background #141413, regardless of theme
Light-mode overrides added: [data-theme='light'] .chat-frame, .chat-chrome, .chat-input-bar all stay dark
User message bubbles have purple accent background (rgba(116, 65, 143, 0.15)) with 1px accent border
Cosimo message bubbles have no background — just text on the dark frame
Cosimo avatar: 24px square, purple-tinted background, 'C' letter, accent border
User avatar: 24px square, cream-ghost background, 'U' letter
Citations styled as small dimmed IBM Plex Mono text with '↳' prefix
Artifact cards have green left border (3px), green-tinted background, file icon + name + meta
Thinking dots animation: 3 small dots pulsing sequentially
Fake input bar at bottom: dark background with placeholder text
Messages have opacity:0 + translateY(8px) default, with .visible class transitioning to full opacity
Mobile responsive rules added: at 640px, tabs scroll horizontally, chat body padding reduces, bubble max-width increases to 85%
Purple accent border gradient on top of frame (same as old terminal)
userStories.notes:
Reference these design specs:
- Frame bg: #141413 (always dark)
- Chrome bg: #1A1A18
- Text color: #EDE8DF (cream) at various opacities
- Accent: #74418F
- Green for artifacts: #2ECC71
- Font: IBM Plex Mono for chrome/labels, Space Grotesk for message body
- chat-body: min-height 420px, max-height 500px, overflow-y auto with thin scrollbar
- chat-msg has 1.25rem gap between messages
- Bubble max-width: 75% desktop, 85% mobile

userStories.title: Replace terminal HTML with chat UI markup
userStories.description: Replace the terminal section HTML in index.html with the new chat UI structure. Keep the same section wrapper and intro text.
acceptanceCriteria: Section still has class 'terminal-section' and id 'product'
Intro header preserved: left side has h2 'Ask anything. Get the work delivered.' (with <em> on 'work'), right side has 'COSIMO — Applied Intelligence / Live product · Not a mockup'
Old terminal-frame div replaced with new chat-frame div containing: chat-chrome (with term-dots, chat-tabs with 3 buttons, chat-version), chat-body (empty div with id='chat-body'), chat-input-bar
Three tab buttons: 'Due Diligence' (data-demo='0'), 'LP Reporting' (data-demo='1'), 'Rent Roll' (data-demo='2'). First tab has 'active' class
Version label reads 'cosimo v2.4.1'
Below the chat-frame, a div with class 'chat-try-cta' containing: Try Cosimo with sample fund data →
Old terminal-body div completely removed
No inline onclick handlers — tab interactivity will be handled by JS in a later story
userStories.notes: ONLY change the content inside <section class='terminal-section' id='product'>. Do NOT change any other section. The .chat-frame replaces .terminal-frame INSIDE the existing .terminal-stage wrapper div — keep that wrapper. Add data-lenis-prevent attribute to the .chat-body div so Lenis smooth scroll doesn't interfere with scrolling inside the chat area.

userStories.title: Replace terminal JS with chat demo logic
userStories.description: Replace the terminal demo JavaScript in js/main.js with the new chat conversation playback system.
acceptanceCriteria: Old code REMOVED: the entire 'demos' array, the 'demoRunning' variable, the 'runDemo()' function, and the ScrollTrigger that called runDemo(0)
New 'chatDemos' array with 3 demo objects, each containing a 'context' string and a 'messages' array
Demo 0 (Due Diligence): context='Alpine Capital III · 847 documents · 2.4GB indexed', 4 messages about co-invest obligations ending with a generated PDF artifact
Demo 1 (LP Reporting): context='Alpine Capital III · Q3 2025 reporting cycle', 4 messages about quarterly performance summary ending with LP letter artifact
Demo 2 (Rent Roll): context='Summit RE Holdings · 8 properties · 23 rent roll PDFs uploaded', 4 messages about rent roll extraction ending with Excel artifact
Each Cosimo response includes 2-3 citations (file names with page/section references)
Each demo ends with an artifact card (icon, filename, meta description)

New 'runChatDemo(index)' function that: clears chat body, shows context indicator, then plays messages sequentially with: user messages fade in immediately, thinking dots appear before Cosimo responses (1.2s), Cosimo responses fade in with citations and artifacts
Tab click handlers: querySelectorAll('.chat-tab') with click listeners calling runChatDemo()
ScrollTrigger auto-starts demo 0 when .chat-frame enters viewport at 70%
Tab active state toggles correctly on click
chatRunning flag prevents overlapping demos
Messages use requestAnimationFrame + .visible class for smooth fade-in (not GSAP — keep it vanilla for this component)
userStories.notes: IMPORTANT: Keep ALL other existing JS code intact — the hero animations, scroll reveals for other sections, word-by-word header reveals, parallax, theme toggle, Lenis setup. Only remove/replace the terminal demo code (the 'demos' array, 'demoRunning' var, 'runDemo' function, and the ScrollTrigger.create that triggers on '.terminal-frame'). These are at the END of main.js after the splitWordsAndAnimate calls and arch-header-num parallax. The GSAP reveal for .terminal-stage (around line 100-104) should be LEFT INTACT — it still works because the new .chat-frame lives inside .terminal-stage.

userStories.title: Clean up old terminal CSS
userStories.description: Remove CSS rules from styles.css that targeted the old terminal elements which no longer exist in the HTML.
acceptanceCriteria: REMOVED: .terminal-frame, .terminal-frame::before, .term-chrome, .term-tabs, .term-tab, .term-tab.active, .term-tab:hover:not(.active), .term-version, .term-body, .term-body.active, .term-line, .term-line.visible, .t-cursor, .cursor-blink, @keyframes blink
REMOVED: color utility classes .p, .c, .d, .r, .g, .s, .gap (these were terminal-specific)

REMOVED: ALL [data-theme='light'] overrides for terminal classes — .terminal-frame, .term-chrome, .term-tab, .term-tab.active, .term-tab:hover, .term-version, .term-body, .p, .c, .d, .r, .g, .s, .t-cursor, .cursor-blink
KEPT: .terminal-section, .terminal-intro, .terminal-intro-left, .terminal-intro-right, .terminal-stage — these wrapper classes are still used
KEPT: .term-dots, .term-dot — these are reused in the chat chrome
No visual regressions — the chat demo CSS from US-002 handles all new styling
userStories.notes: Be careful not to remove CSS for elements that are still in use. The .term-dots and .term-dot classes are reused in the new chat chrome. When in doubt, search index.html for the class name before removing its CSS.

userStories.title: Rewrite pain section as before/after comparison
userStories.description: Replace the stats grid in the pain section with a two-column 'Today vs. With Cosimo' comparison layout showing quarterly LP reporting workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Pain section label kept as 'The Status Quo'

Headline changed to: 'One quarterly LP report.
Here's what it actually takes.'
Old 3-column stats grid (.pain-grid with .pain-items) completely removed from HTML
New two-column comparison layout: .pain-comparison container with .pain-col.pain-before, .pain-divider, .pain-col.pain-after

Left column ('Today') has 6 specific steps describing the manual LP reporting process (pulling NAV data, cross-referencing spreadsheets, extracting from PDFs, drafting in Word, partner review cycle, individual LP statements) plus summary: '3 people. 3 weeks. Every quarter.'
Right column ('With Cosimo') has 3 steps (ask Cosimo, it pulls/reconciles/formats, you review with source citations) plus summary: '1 person. 15 minutes. Same report.'
Purple vertical divider between columns
Column labels styled as IBM Plex Mono uppercase — left label in cream-dim, right label in accent color
Steps have left border (subtle for 'Today', accent-tinted for 'With Cosimo')
Summary lines styled differently — IBM Plex Mono with top border separator
New CSS added: .pain-comparison (3-column grid), .pain-col, .pain-before, .pain-after, .pain-divider, .pain-col-header, .pain-col-label, .pain-col-body, .pain-step, .pain-step-summary
Responsive: columns stack vertically at 768px, divider becomes horizontal
Old CSS removed: .pain-grid, .pain-item, .pain-num, .pain-desc
GSAP animation in main.js updated: remove .pain-item animation, add staggered reveal for .pain-col elements

userStories.title: Rewrite architecture section copy
userStories.description: Rewrite the three architecture card titles and descriptions to focus on buyer trust, not technical implementation.
acceptanceCriteria:
Header h2 changed to: 'Three things generic AI
will never do for your fund.'

Card 1 title: 'Your Data Stays Yours' — text about data segregation, proprietary processes never touching other clients, competitive advantage staying theirs. Mention it's architecture not just policy.
Card 2 title: 'It Gets Smarter at Your Work' — text about compounding knowledge over months (month 1 vs month 6 vs month 12 narrative), the moat story

Card 3 title: 'The Work, Done' — text listing specific deliverables (LP letter, K-1 package, rent roll extraction, compliance report), emphasize source-cited and audit-ready, 'not analysis, not insights — the actual deliverable'
Card numbers (001, 002, 003) unchanged
Card icons (◇, ⟳, ▣) unchanged
ALL HTML structure unchanged — same classes, same nesting. Only text content inside .arch-card-title and .arch-card-text divs changes
No CSS changes needed
userStories.notes: ONLY edit index.html inside the .arch-section. Touch nothing else. Tone: confident, adult, specific. These cards should make a Fund CFO think 'they understand my concerns.'

userStories.title: Rewrite quotes with specificity and add framing line
userStories.description: Add a framing line above the quotes grid and rewrite all three quotes with more operational specificity and detailed attributions.
acceptanceCriteria:
New

element added inside .quotes-section, ABOVE .quotes-grid, with text: 'We don't name our clients. They prefer it that way.'
CSS for .quotes-framing: IBM Plex Mono, 0.6rem, letter-spacing 0.15em, uppercase, accent-colored, centered, margin-bottom 3rem, opacity 0.7

Quote 1 rewritten: about K-1 prep going from 3 weeks to 2 days, analyst using extra time to reconcile waterfall discrepancies. Attribution: 'VP of Fund Accounting, $1.2B real estate PE fund, 3-person finance team'
Quote 2 rewritten: about associate threatening to quit, shift from midnight to 7pm during diligence sprints. Attribution: 'Managing Partner, mid-market buyout fund, NYC'

Quote 3 rewritten: about implementation taking 2 days instead of expected 3 months, compliance team querying side letters on day two with source citations. Attribution: 'Director of Operations, $800M credit fund, 4-person ops team'
All existing HTML structure preserved — .quote-card, .quote-mark, .quote-text, .quote-attr elements unchanged structurally
GSAP: add a fade-in animation for .quotes-framing triggered when quotes-section enters viewport
userStories.notes: The quotes should sound like how finance professionals actually talk — direct, slightly dry, operationally specific. NOT marketing copy.

userStories.title: Add founder's note section
userStories.description: Add a new 'From the founders' section between quotes and use cases sections.
acceptanceCriteria: New <section class='founder-section'> inserted in index.html AFTER the closing </section> of .quotes-section and BEFORE the opening of .cases-section
Contains: .founder-content wrapper (max-width 640px), .founder-label ('From the founders'), .founder-text (placeholder paragraph), .founder-sign ('— Eliot & the Medici team')

Placeholder text: 'We built Medici because we sat in the seats our clients sit in. We know what it's like to reconcile a waterfall at 2am because an LP noticed a discrepancy. We know what gets lost when your best analyst leaves. This isn't a technology company that decided to serve finance. It's a finance team that decided to build the infrastructure we wished existed.'

CSS added: .founder-section (padding 5rem 2.5rem, border-top), .founder-content (max-width 640px), .founder-label (IBM Plex Mono, accent-colored, uppercase), .founder-text (Space Grotesk, 1rem, line-height 1.8), .founder-sign (IBM Plex Mono, dimmed)
Mobile CSS: padding reduces at 640px
GSAP scroll reveal for .founder-text (fade in, slide up)
userStories.notes: This section should feel quiet and personal against the more designed sections around it. No cards, no grid — just text.

userStories.title: Rewrite use cases with persona tags
userStories.description: Add persona labels to each use case card and tighten the copy with more specific metrics.
acceptanceCriteria:
Each .case-card has a new first child:
...
— inserted before .case-num
CSS for .case-persona: IBM Plex Mono, 0.5rem, letter-spacing 0.12em, uppercase, accent-colored, margin-bottom 0.5rem, opacity 0.6
Card 01: persona 'For Investment Partners & Associates', title 'Due Diligence', desc rewritten with '1,200-document data room' specificity, stat '2 hrs' with label 'vs. 2 weeks, manual'
Card 02: persona 'For Fund CFOs', title changed to 'K-1s & Regulatory Filing', desc rewritten mentioning K-1 packages and Form ADV, stat 'Days→Hrs' kept with label changed to 'Quarter-end close time'

Card 03: persona 'For IR & Fund Accounting', title changed to 'LP Reporting', desc rewritten with '4pm LP question, 30 second response' scenario, stat changed to '30 sec' with label 'LP query response time'

Card 04: persona 'For Fund CFOs & Partners', title 'Portfolio Monitoring' kept, desc rewritten mentioning covenant compliance and waterfall calculations, stat 'Real-time' kept with label changed to 'Across all portfolio companies'
No changes to card HTML structure beyond adding the persona div as first child
No changes to existing CSS classes for cards
userStories.notes: ONLY edit the content inside .cases-section in index.html, plus add the .case-persona CSS rule. Do not change the cases-header or grid structure.

userStories.title: Move inevitability section and redesign CTA as pilot timeline
userStories.description: Move the 'Every fund will work this way' line to above the CTA, then replace the CTA with a structured 3-step pilot timeline.
acceptanceCriteria: The .inevitability-section is CUT from its current position (between use cases and CTA) and PASTED immediately before the .cta-section — so it now appears after use cases, before CTA
CTA section label changed to 'Start a Pilot'

CTA headline changed to: 'Four weeks. Your data.
You'll know.'
Old .cta-sub and .cta-cred paragraphs REMOVED
New .pilot-timeline div added: 3-column grid (.pilot-step elements) with border treatment matching the architecture cards
Step 1: num '01', duration '30 minutes', title 'Discovery', desc about learning workflows and scoping the pilot
Step 2: num '02', duration '1 week', title 'Onboarding', desc about dedicated segregated instance, configuring for fund's document types
Step 3: num '03', duration '3 weeks', title 'Live Pilot', desc about using on real work, measuring results
New .pilot-confidence paragraph below timeline: 'No annual contract. No integration project. No IT involvement required. If it doesn't change how your fund operates, walk away.'
CTA buttons: primary 'Schedule a Discovery Call' (href='#'), secondary 'hello@medici.ai' mailto link
CSS added: .pilot-timeline (3-col grid, bordered), .pilot-step, .pilot-step-num, .pilot-step-duration, .pilot-step-title, .pilot-step-desc, .pilot-confidence
Responsive: steps stack at 768px
GSAP: staggered reveal for .pilot-step elements, remove old .cta-sub animation
Old CSS for .cta-sub and .cta-cred can be removed

userStories.title: Update meta description and verify nav anchors
userStories.description: Update the meta description to match new positioning, and verify all nav anchor links still work.
acceptanceCriteria: Meta description updated to avoid the word 'AI' and focus on outcomes. Under 160 characters. Should mention fund operations and specific deliverable types.
Nav link #product still correctly targets the terminal/chat demo section (id='product')
Nav link #architecture still correctly targets the arch section (id='architecture')
Nav link #cases still correctly targets the use cases section (id='cases')
Nav link #contact still correctly targets the CTA section (id='contact')
Verify that .chat-body element has data-lenis-prevent attribute (should have been added in US-003 — add it if missing)
userStories.notes: Quick housekeeping task. Should be very fast.

userStories.title: Final GSAP animation audit and cleanup
userStories.description: Verify all GSAP animations target elements that exist, remove any orphaned animations, and add missing animations for new sections.
acceptanceCriteria: NO GSAP animation targets a class/element that doesn't exist in index.html
Specifically check and fix: .pain-item animations removed (element doesn't exist), .cta-sub animation removed (element doesn't exist)

New animations exist for: .quotes-framing (fade in), .founder-text (fade in, slide up), .pain-col (staggered reveal), .pilot-step (staggered reveal), .chat-frame (fade in, slide up via existing .terminal-stage trigger)
All ScrollTriggers use once: true
All animations use power3.out easing (matching existing pattern)
Word-by-word reveal function still targets elements that exist (.terminal-intro-left h2, .pain-headline, .arch-header h2, .cases-header h2)
The .pain-headline word animation still works even though the headline text changed
No console errors when scrolling through the full page (verify by reading the JS logic)
userStories.notes: Read through ALL of main.js carefully. Cross-reference every CSS selector in the JS with the actual HTML. This is the final verification pass.
